name: Measuring Reaction Rates
description: Reaction rates are measured by tracking a concentration-dependent observable over time: the technique is chosen by reaction speed (stopped-flow mixing for fast reactions, periodic sampling for slow ones), the recorded signal is converted to concentration, and tangents or integrated fits yield the instantaneous rate.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Reacting system"]
    N2["Pick observable signal"]
    N3{"Fast or slow reaction?"}
    N4["Fast: stopped-flow"]
    N5["Slow: periodic sampling"]
    N6["Record signal vs time"]
    N7["Convert signal to conc"]
    N8["Compute rate"]
    N9["Tangent / integrated fit"]
    N10["Reaction rate vs time"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 -->|fast| N4
    N3 -->|slow| N5
    N4 --> N6
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
